name: benomyl

citations.detail: EPA SRS/CompTox substance record giving benomyl DTXSID5023900 and CAS 17804-35-2 (C14H18N4O3, MW 290.32).
Wikidata cross-reference confirming DSSTox DTXSID5023900 for benomyl (InChIKey RIOXQFHNBCKOKP-UHFFFAOYSA-N).

Fitzmaurice et al. PNAS 2013: benomyl, via its thiocarbamate-sulfoxide metabolite, inhibits ALDH, causing DOPAL accumulation and selective dopaminergic neuronal damage; higher benomyl exposure associated with increased PD risk in their epidemiology study.

Fitzmaurice et al. Neurology 2014 (PEG Study): benomyl among ALDH-inhibiting pesticides; ALDH2 variants modify pesticide-associated PD risk. Authors caution against attributing causation to a single pesticide due to correlated exposures.
Legge et al. (Chem Res Toxicol 2015, tx5002223): benomyl potently inhibits ALDH and builds up DOPAL in vivo (mouse striatum) and in vitro; supports the catecholaldehyde hypothesis for PD.

Fernandez-Hubeid et al. (2024, C. elegans): proposes ALDH/ALH inhibition as a molecular initiating event (MIE) in an adverse outcome pathway of benomyl neurotoxicity relevant to parkinsonism — an AOP described in literature, not confirmed as a registered AOP-Wiki entry.
CTD toxicogenomics-grouping paper notes benomyl was linked in CTD to prenatal injuries but annotated only in zebrafish; no PD DirectEvidence entry could be substantiated here.

notes: Identity (DTXSID5023900 / CAS 17804-35-2, free base; benomyl is a benzimidazole carbamate fungicide, not a salt) is well substantiated via EPA SRS and Wikidata. pdDirect set to 0: I could NOT directly reach ctdbase.org to confirm a CURATED CTD Parkinson's Disease DirectEvidence (marker/mechanism or therapeutic) association for benomyl; searches did not return a CTD chemical-disease page, and one paper indicates benomyl's CTD disease annotations were limited (prenatal injury, zebrafish-only). A human MUST query CTD directly (http://ctdbase.org, chemical benomyl, MESH D001525) and check the 'Direct Evidence' column for 'Parkinson Disease' before setting pdDirect=1. aopStressorOf left EMPTY: a benomyl→ALDH-inhibition→dopaminergic-loss AOP is described in the primary literature (Fernandez-Hubeid 2024; Fitzmaurice PNAS 2013), but I could not confirm benomyl is a REGISTERED stressor on any OECD AOP-Wiki neuro AOP; verify at https://aopwiki.org (search 'benomyl' and ALDH/complex-I/parkinsonian AOPs, e.g. potential ALDH-inhibition AOPs). mechActiveTotal and mitoActive set to 0 because I could not retrieve benomyl's ToxCast/Tox21 assay hit counts from the CompTox Dashboard; verify at https://comptox.epa.gov/dashboard/chemical/invitrodb/DTXSID5023900 (Bioactivity tab). Epidemiology is genuine and among the strongest for any PD-linked fungicide, but the original authors explicitly warn that co-exposure to correlated ALDH-inhibiting pesticides (e.g., maneb, ziram, mancozeb) precludes isolating benomyl as causal. Note benomyl was deregistered by US EPA (~2001) and in the EU.
